steps.title: Identify the antecedent
steps.content: The first sentence introduces Creek Council Tree (1967) and Cimarron Iron Bridge (1972) as the subject—two paintings that the second sentence must refer to.

steps.title: Apply pronoun-antecedent agreement
steps.content: Pronouns must match their antecedents in number. “They were” is correct: “they” and “were” are both plural, matching the antecedent. The other choices don't work—”this” and “it” are singular pronouns, and “some” provides indefinite reference instead of clear pronoun reference. That matches choice A — A is correct.
